title: Tangy Velvety Portuguese Spaghetti Toast
description: A quick, adventurous breakfast that pairs toasted sourdough with al dente spaghetti tossed in a citrusy piri‑piri tomato sauce, finished with smoked cheese and fresh herbs.

ingredients.name: shredded smoked cheese (e.g., queijo da Serra)

instructions: Preheat oven or toaster to medium‑high heat and toast the sourdough slices until golden on both sides; set aside.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and cook the spaghetti until al dente (about 8‑9 minutes); drain and reserve 30 ml of pasta water.
In a small saucepan, heat 15 ml olive oil over medium heat and sauté the minced garlic until fragrant (≈30 s).
Add the smoked paprika and piri‑piri powder, cooking for another 30 s to bloom the spices.
Stir in the crushed tomatoes, lemon juice, and lemon zest; let the mixture simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Return the cooked spaghetti to the pan, adding a splash of reserved pasta water to loosen the sauce. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the spaghetti mixture to a bowl, top with shredded smoked cheese and chopped parsley.
Arrange the toasted bread slices on a plate, spoon the warm spaghetti mixture onto each slice, drizzle the remaining olive oil, and serve immediately.
